vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not clearly identifiable as healthy, living plants. The scene is dominated by dark, low-light conditions and soil-filled containers.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears to contain only substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No visible foliage; pot appears to contain only substrate.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent or non-viable.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; no plant matter visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp-appearing across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: A spoon is present in the yellow pot (P1/P2 area) in T-3 and T-2; a white cylindrical object (possibly a sensor or marker) appears in T-2 and persists through current.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though low light limits resolution.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Minimal change; pots remain largely devoid of visible vegetation.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Introduction of a spoon and a cylindrical marker/sensor in the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_current: The scene remains static; no growth or recovery observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of total vegetative failure. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue suggests the specimens have either been removed, died back completely, or are buried beneath the soil surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. My methodology involved first cataloging the expected registry against the visual data, followed by a frame-by-frame analysis to detect changes. The audit confirms that the expected plants are not visible. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed indoor setup. The introduction of non-botanical items (spoon, sensor) suggests human intervention, but no biological recovery has occurred. The biome is currently non-productive.
